---
# GENERATED by my-dev-team-config/scripts/sync_partials.py - edit partials/code-style.md there, not this file.
name: code-style
description: Write code that fits the codebase - style, dependency and secrets hygiene.
---

- Write code that fits the file it lives in: match the surrounding naming,
  idiom and comment density rather than imposing a different style. New code
  should read as if the existing author wrote it. Do not add narration
  comments that restate what the code plainly does; a comment earns its place
  only by explaining intent, a trade-off or a non-obvious constraint.
- Do not assume a library, framework or tool is available. Before you use a
  dependency, confirm the project already has it - check its manifest
  (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod and the like) or an existing
  import - and prefer what the codebase already uses over introducing
  something new.
- Never hardcode a secret - an API key, token, password or connection
  string - into code or config. Read secrets from environment variables or
  the project's existing configuration mechanism; if one is genuinely
  missing, say so in your report rather than inventing or embedding a value.
